docs.text:
Show / hide

Translational neoadjuvant with paired biopsies — well-designed for PD question; small n.

docs.title: Confidence — rationale
docs.text:
Show / hide

Full text; multi-marker repolarization evidence is stronger than CD163-only single-marker IHC that other rows sometimes rely on.

docs.title: Retraction status
docs.text:
Show / hide

none

docs.title: Weiss SA 2021 — critique
docs.text:
A Phase I Study of APX005M and Cabiralizumab with or without Nivolumab in Patients with Melanoma, Kidney Cancer, or Non-Small Cell Lung Cancer Resistant to Anti-PD-1/PD-L1

← back to critique report · ← back to shieldbreak

PMID: 34140403 · PMCID: PMC9236708 · DOI: 10.1158/1078-0432.CCR-21-0903

Risk of bias (ROBINS-I): Moderate Per-trial confidence: Moderate Counter-productive MoA: High (cytokine-release, antigen-presentation-collateral, tissue-macrophage-collateral) Source tier: pmc_full_text

docs.title: Key critique
docs.text:
Show / hide

Randomized phase 1 of APX005M (sotigalimab, CD40 agonist) + cabiralizumab (anti-CSF1R) ± nivolumab in advanced PDAC (NCT03214250). Negative efficacy (ORR ~0%); PBMC PD confirms target engagement. This trial is one of the clearest examples in the set of 'target hit, tumor unmoved' — important for the overall skepticism calibration on CSF1R-based combinations in PDAC.

docs.title: Per-dimension findings
docs.text: Show / hide

docs.title: Design
docs.text: Randomized phase 1, two arms (triplet vs doublet), PDAC post-first-line chemotherapy. n=28 treated across arms.

docs.title: Sample size
docs.text: Small phase 1 with randomization to explore safety of triplet vs doublet; not powered for efficacy.

docs.title: Effect magnitude calibration
docs.text: Effectively null response signal — consistent with the cabiralizumab + nivolumab PDAC phase 2 failure (Wainberg 2021).

docs.title: Missing data
docs.text: Reported per protocol.

docs.title: Multiplicity
docs.text: Two arms, descriptive.

docs.title: Generalizability
docs.text: Second-line PDAC — a setting where nothing has worked in TAM-modulation trials.

docs.title: Gating / definition
docs.text: PBMC flow cytometry for monocyte subsets; limited paired tumor biopsies.

docs.title: Counter-productive mechanisms
docs.text: Triple immune modulation (CSF1R + CD40 + PD-1) risks over-activating systemic immunity without productive tumor-antigen engagement. CSF1R blockade may remove antigen-presenting macrophages that CD40 agonism is trying to activate — a plausible mechanistic conflict. PDAC's desmoplasia and low TMB compound the challenge.

docs.title: Conflict of interest & funding
docs.text: Apexigen (APX005M), Five Prime (cabiralizumab), BMS (nivolumab) — three-sponsor trial.

docs.title: Spin / framing
docs.text: Honest about lack of clinical activity.

docs.title: Extraction fidelity
docs.text:
Show / hide

No discrepancies noted — the screener's extracted values match the source.

docs.title: Risk of bias — rationale
docs.text:
Show / hide

Randomized phase 1 with arm comparison — better than typical single-arm phase 1, still limited by sample and open-label design.

docs.title: Confidence — rationale
docs.text:
Show / hide

Full-text; negative efficacy is the most informative finding — CSF1R + CD40 combination hypothesis has now been tested and failed in PDAC.

docs.title: Retraction status
docs.text:
Show / hide

none

docs.title: Manji GA 2021 — critique
docs.text:
A Phase I Study of the Combination of Pexidartinib and Sirolimus to Target Tumor-Associated Macrophages in Unresectable Sarcoma and Malignant Peripheral Nerve Sheath Tumors

← back to critique report · ← back to shieldbreak

PMID: 34321280 · PMCID: PMC8530953 · DOI: 10.1158/1078-0432.CCR-21-1779

Risk of bias (ROBINS-I): Serious Per-trial confidence: Low Counter-productive MoA: Moderate (hepatotoxicity, lymphopenia-collateral, tissue-macrophage-collateral, mtor-effector-suppression) Source tier: pmc_full_text

docs.title: Key critique
docs.text:
Show / hide

Small phase 1 TITE-CRM combination of pexidartinib + sirolimus in advanced sarcoma. Clinical benefit concentrated in TGCT (where CSF1R monotherapy already works). The qmIF TAM readout is based on only 3 patients with paired pre/on-treatment tumor tissue (4 paired samples, 1 excluded for no neoplastic cells) and the decline in activated M2 (CD206+HLA-DR+CD68+) is described in the text as 'non-statistically significant trends.' The screener's row lists n=18 with CD68-CD163-based gating and depletion_success: succeeded — which materially overstates what the paper supports.

docs.title: Per-dimension findings
docs.text: Show / hide

docs.title: Design
docs.text: Multicenter phase 1, TITE-CRM dose-finding, n=24 enrolled (18 evaluable), 5 dose levels of pexidartinib (600–1000 mg) + sirolimus (2–6 mg). NCT02584647. Paired tumor tissue (n≤4) with qmIF on CD68/CD163/CD206/HLA-DR/CSF1R/CD8. RP2D 1000 mg pexidartinib + 2 mg sirolimus.

docs.title: Sample size
docs.text: 18 evaluable for clinical endpoints; n≈3 for the TAM qmIF readout. Reductions in activated M2 described as 'non-statistically significant trends' in the text.

docs.title: Effect magnitude calibration
docs.text: 3 PRs all in TGCT — consistent with pexidartinib monotherapy in TGCT. For sarcoma subtypes (MPNST, LMS) the mPFS gain is exploratory and uncontrolled.

docs.title: Missing data
docs.text: 6 nonevaluable of 24 enrolled (25%) — nonadherence, withdrawal, progression pre-cycle-1, SAE unlikely drug-related. Of paired biopsies, 1 excluded for absent neoplastic cells.

docs.title: Multiplicity
docs.text: Multiple marker panels (CD206, HLA-DR, CD163, CD68, CSF1R, CD8) descriptively compared with no adjustment.

docs.title: Generalizability
docs.text: Mixed sarcoma subtypes; TGCT drives the efficacy signal. Generalizing to non-TGCT sarcoma or other solid tumors is premature.

docs.title: Gating / definition
docs.text: Quantitative multiplex immunofluorescence (qmIF) on CD8/CD68/HLA-DR/CD163/CD206/CSF1R with inForm segmentation — a strong gating strategy (should be classed mIF-multiplex in schema). Activated M2 defined as CD68+CD206+HLA-DR+.

docs.title: Counter-productive mechanisms
docs.text: Pexidartinib is a TKI with broader kinase footprint (CSF1R, KIT, FLT3-ITD) than the anti-CSF1R mAbs — hepatic toxicity led to black-box warning. Combined with sirolimus, lymphopenia is a DLT-level concern. The counter-productive angle here is the combination itself: mTOR inhibition suppresses effector T-cell proliferation while the TAM readout is being measured, which could bias immune-milieu conclusions.

docs.title: Conflict of interest & funding
docs.text: Daiichi Sankyo supplied pexidartinib; NIH/institutional funding. Authors disclose various industry relationships.

docs.title: Spin / framing
docs.text: Paper does not overclaim — uses 'non-statistically significant trends' and 'support further investigation.' However, the screener's summary inherits the optimistic abstract phrasing ('decreased proportion of activated M2 macrophages with treatment') and reads harder than the figure legend reads.

docs.title: Extraction fidelity
docs.text:
Show / hide
  • n_treated_with_tam_measurement: source says —; extraction has 18.
  • gating_quality: source says —; extraction has CD68-CD163-based.
  • change_mechanism: source says —; extraction has absolute-reduction.
  • depletion_success: source says —; extraction has succeeded.

docs.title: Risk of bias — rationale
docs.text:
Show / hide

Small uncontrolled phase 1 with a 3-patient PD readout showing non-significant trends; the extracted claim of 'succeeded' overstates the evidence.

docs.title: Confidence — rationale
docs.text:
Show / hide

Full-text read reveals the PD claim rests on ~3 patients with non-significant trends; the row should be downgraded from succeeded to partial or not-assessed.

docs.title: Retraction status
docs.text:
Show / hide

none

docs.title: Meric-Bernstam F 2022 — critique
docs.text:
Phase I Dose-Escalation Trial of MIW815 (ADU-S100), an Intratumoral STING Agonist, in Patients with Advanced/Metastatic Solid Tumors or Lymphomas

← back to critique report · ← back to shieldbreak

PMID: 34716197 · DOI: 10.1158/1078-0432.CCR-21-1963

Risk of bias (ROBINS-I): Moderate Per-trial confidence: Moderate Counter-productive MoA: High (ifn-driven-treg-expansion, ido1-induction, pd-l1-induction, checkpoint-upregulation) Source tier: pubmed_abstract

docs.title: Key critique
docs.text:
Show / hide

First-in-human STING agonist (MIW815/ADU-S100) intratumoral injection in 47 patients. 'RNA expression and immune infiltration assessments in paired tumor biopsies did not reveal significant on-treatment changes.' Single-digit PR rate. This is the null-result paper for the STING-agonist first-generation hypothesis. The screener's row should reflect this.

docs.title: Per-dimension findings
docs.text: Show / hide

docs.title: Design
docs.text: Phase 1 dose-escalation, IT MIW815 50-6400 µg on 3-weeks-on/1-week-off. n=47. Paired tumor biopsy analyses.

docs.title: Sample size
docs.text: n=47 total; paired biopsy subset smaller. Adequate to rule out large on-treatment effects.

docs.title: Effect magnitude calibration
docs.text: Confirmed PR in 1/47 (Merkel), 2 unconfirmed PRs. Lesion size stable/decreased in 94% of injected lesions — but typical of intratumoral injection irrespective of drug. Systemic immune activation in peripheral blood but NOT in tumor.

docs.title: Missing data
docs.text: Not extractable from abstract.

docs.title: Multiplicity
docs.text: Multiple biomarker readouts — none statistically significant.

docs.title: Generalizability
docs.text: Heavily pretreated advanced/metastatic solid tumors/lymphomas.

docs.title: Gating / definition
docs.text: RNA expression + immune cell IHC infiltration scores in paired biopsies. No TAM-specific gating mentioned in abstract.

docs.title: Counter-productive mechanisms
docs.text: This is a textbook example of the STING-agonist counter-productive-mechanism hypothesis: systemic Type-I IFN induces Treg expansion, IDO1 induction, PD-L1 upregulation — all of which counterbalance the effector arm. The 'activated effectors but no tumor control' peripheral/local dissociation in this abstract is precisely the pattern that the user's spec seed flagged.

docs.title: Conflict of interest & funding
docs.text: Novartis/Aduro; not in abstract.

docs.title: Spin / framing
docs.text: Honest — reports the null tumor finding clearly.

docs.title: Extraction fidelity
docs.text:
Show / hide

No discrepancies noted — the screener's extracted values match the source.

docs.title: Risk of bias — rationale
docs.text:
Show / hide

Standard phase 1 IT design; null finding robustly reported.

docs.title: Confidence — rationale
docs.text:
Show / hide

Abstract-only but the null tumor-biomarker result IS the paper's main contribution and is clearly reported.

docs.title: Retraction status
docs.text:
Show / hide

none

The ADU-S100 program was effectively discontinued after this and similar trials; MK-1454 (Merck) and other first-gen STING agonists also failed to replicate preclinical promise.

docs.title: Gomez-Roca C 2022 — critique
docs.text:
Anti-CSF-1R emactuzumab in combination with anti-PD-L1 atezolizumab in advanced solid tumor patients naive or experienced for immune checkpoint blockade

← back to critique report · ← back to shieldbreak

PMID: 35577503 · PMCID: PMC9114963 · DOI: 10.1136/jitc-2021-004076

Risk of bias (ROBINS-I): Moderate Per-trial confidence: High Counter-productive MoA: High (depletes-beneficial-effectors, tissue-macrophage-collateral, antigen-presentation-collateral, csf1-rebound) Source tier: pmc_full_text

docs.title: Key critique
docs.text:
Show / hide

Large phase 1b (N=221) with the most complete TAM PD dataset in the set. Marked reduction of CSF1R+ and CD163+ TAMs in paired tumor biopsies (median −80% CSF1R+, −63% CD163+ in non-PD patients; CSF1R+ −72% / CD163+ −28% in non-PD — the paper itself notes the counterintuitive direction: less TAM reduction was associated with clinical benefit). Clinical activity is modest (ORR 9.8% UBC-naïve, 12.5% NSCLC ICB-experienced). The authors' own finding that TAM persistence tracked with CD8 expansion is an important negative-for-the-hypothesis result worth flagging.

docs.title: Per-dimension findings
docs.text: Show / hide

docs.title: Design
docs.text: Phase 1b, open-label, non-randomized, multicenter (NCT02323191) with 3+3 dose escalation up to OBD 1000 mg emactuzumab + 1200 mg atezolizumab q3w, then expansion cohorts in ICB-naïve UBC and ICB-experienced MEL/NSCLC/UBC. Paired tumor biopsies at screening + C2D1. Multiplex IHC (CSF1R, CD163/CD68, Ki67/CD8, FOXP3, PD-L1).

docs.title: Sample size
docs.text: N=221 treated overall; PD analyses limited to biopsy-paired subsets (e.g., UBC ICB-naïve n≈41 but only subset had evaluable paired biopsies). Expansion cohorts of 18–41 across the 4 groups are adequate for PD but under-powered for definitive efficacy.

docs.title: Effect magnitude calibration
docs.text: ORRs track the lower end of atezolizumab monotherapy in each setting (UBC-naïve 9.8% vs ~15% historical; NSCLC ICB-experienced 12.5% is arguably above background but in a small cohort). TAM reductions of 70–80% are among the largest in this shieldbreak.

docs.title: Missing data
docs.text: Paired-biopsy attrition substantial (exact n per comparison variable and shown in Fig 3 but subject to selection). 34 patients discontinued due to AE.

docs.title: Multiplicity
docs.text: Multiple cohorts × multiple biomarkers × multiple subgroup comparisons (PD vs non-PD) without multiplicity adjustment — explicitly acknowledged by authors ('Wilcoxon rank sums test' at α=0.05 per comparison).

docs.title: Generalizability
docs.text: Mix of tumor types and ICI-status strata; results are hypothesis-generating per cohort.

docs.title: Gating / definition
docs.text: Dual-marker IHC (CD163+CD68+; separately CSF1R+) with Ki67/CD8 co-stain and FOXP3 stain for Tregs. Single-marker CD163 is reported alongside CSF1R — the combined readout is stronger than either alone. No mIF multiplex or CyTOF; quantitation is per-mm² density on 2.5 µm sections.

docs.title: Counter-productive mechanisms
docs.text: This paper itself documents a plausible counter-productive mechanism: deeper CSF1R+/CD163+ TAM reduction was associated with PD (progression), and persistence of a TAM sub-population correlated with CD8 expansion and clinical benefit. The interpretation the authors propose is that some TAMs provide antigen-presentation support required for CD8 priming, and broad CSF1R depletion removes those supportive macrophages too. Classic CSF1R collateral (skin macrophages, periorbital edema in 28.5%; elevated LFTs) is also present. This is a concrete example of the 'depleting TAMs can remove antigen-presenting support' hypothesis.

docs.title: Conflict of interest & funding
docs.text: Roche/Genentech funded. Multiple authors are employees (WJ, MAC, CR, KK, FM, RC, GB, CW, GM-L, MW, DR). Clinical investigators report extensive industry ties including Roche/Genentech/BMS. First draft written by employees plus corresponding author — transparent.

docs.title: Spin / framing
docs.text: Discussion frames the TAM-persistence/CD8 correlation as 'association with clinical benefit', which is accurate; the abstract's 'increase of CD8+TILs under therapy appeared to be associated with persistence of a TAM subpopulation' is a notably honest phrasing for a sponsor-led paper reporting against the primary hypothesis.

docs.title: Extraction fidelity
docs.text:
Show / hide

No discrepancies noted — the screener's extracted values match the source.

docs.title: Risk of bias — rationale
docs.text:
Show / hide

Largest phase 1b in the set with paired biopsies and independent radiology; non-randomized comparisons (PD vs non-PD) are susceptible to confounding by baseline TAM density and prior ICB.

docs.title: Confidence — rationale
docs.text:
Show / hide

Full text available; the PD finding is robust and the honest reporting of the counterintuitive TAM/response relationship raises confidence in the paper's methodological integrity.

docs.title: Retraction status
docs.text:
Show / hide

none

docs.title: Kitko CL 2023 — critique
docs.text:
Axatilimab for Chronic Graft-Versus-Host Disease after failure of at least two prior lines of systemic therapy: Results of a Phase I/II study

← back to critique report · ← back to shieldbreak

PMID: 36459673 · PMCID: PMC10082302 · DOI: 10.1200/JCO.22.00958

Risk of bias (ROBINS-I): Moderate Per-trial confidence: Moderate Counter-productive MoA: Moderate (tissue-macrophage-collateral, csf1-rebound, il34-escape, osteoclast-depletion) Source tier: pubmed_abstract

docs.title: Key critique
docs.text:
Show / hide

Clearest human CSF1R-depletion proof-of-mechanism to date — but the indication is cGVHD, not cancer. The abstract shows a 50% primary-endpoint ORR at cycle 7 (first-6-cycles ORR 82%) with decreased skin CSF1R+ macrophages confirming on-target activity. The full text is publisher-restricted on PMC (confirmed in the PMC XML). Caveats for this shieldbreak: (1) this is the clinical validation of the mechanism but in a profibrotic-macrophage context, not a tumor immunosuppressive-TAM context; (2) in an oncology re-use frame, the same tissue-macrophage collateral applies.

docs.title: Per-dimension findings
docs.text: Show / hide

docs.title: Design
docs.text: Phase 1/2 open-label multicenter (NCT03604692). Phase 1: OBD/RP2D-finding across 3 mg/kg q4w and q2w schedules with standard DLT assessment. Phase 2: prespecified primary efficacy endpoint was ORR at cycle 7 day 1 by NIH consensus response measures. Single-arm; no randomization. 40 enrolled, all received ≥1 dose. This is a tighter design than most cancer TAM trials in the set — clear phase 1→2 structure with a pre-specified primary endpoint.

docs.title: Sample size
docs.text: n=40 (17 phase 1 + 23 phase 2). Phase 2 was powered to the NIH response definition; 50% ORR met the primary endpoint. Sample is small for subgroup claims but adequate for the primary efficacy hypothesis in a refractory setting.

docs.title: Effect magnitude calibration
docs.text: 50% ORR and especially the 82% first-six-cycles ORR are large for refractory cGVHD (median 4 prior lines per the abstract). This led to FDA approval of axatilimab (Niktimvo) for cGVHD in 2024 — the efficacy signal replicated in AGAVE-201.

docs.title: Missing data
docs.text: Cannot assess without full text. 40 patients all receiving ≥1 dose suggests minimal attrition for the primary-endpoint analysis, but NIH-ORR definition's completion requirements matter.

docs.title: Multiplicity
docs.text: ORR at cycle 7 was the pre-specified primary; first-6-cycles ORR is framed as 'supporting regulatory approvals.' I'd note that reporting two ORR endpoints invites selective framing, but both favored the drug, so the concern is theoretical here.

docs.title: Generalizability
docs.text: Highly refractory cGVHD after ≥2 prior systemic therapies. Fibrotic-macrophage biology in cGVHD is the target — the translation to tumor TAM depletion (non-fibrotic, immunosuppressive) is mechanistically related but not identical.

docs.title: Gating / definition
docs.text: Skin biopsy CSF1R+ macrophage staining is the PD readout cited in the abstract ('decrease in skin CSF1R-expressing macrophages'). Single-marker skin IHC is a decent mechanistic confirmation for CSF1R engagement but does not multiplex polarization state.

docs.title: Counter-productive mechanisms
docs.text: In cGVHD the on-label goal IS macrophage depletion. In an oncology-repurposing frame: (1) osteoclast depletion (CSF1R-dependent) → fracture risk in long-term dosing; (2) Kupffer-cell depletion → periorbital edema, transaminitis (noted in the abstract as 'known on-target effects'); (3) IL-34-driven TAMs are a CSF1R-independent escape route that should be expected if this drug is ported to oncology. The abstract does not report CSF1 plasma rebound but it is a reproducible class signal.

docs.title: Conflict of interest & funding
docs.text: Syndax Pharmaceuticals employees among authors. Funded by Syndax. Disclosures not in the PMC abstract record (full text suppressed).

docs.title: Spin / framing
docs.text: Abstract is factual — on-target activity 'suggested by' skin macrophage decrease is appropriately hedged.

docs.title: Extraction fidelity
docs.text:
Show / hide

No discrepancies noted — the screener's extracted values match the source.

docs.title: Risk of bias — rationale
docs.text:
Show / hide

Single-arm phase 1/2 with pre-specified primary endpoint and objective NIH consensus measures; confounding and placebo/expectation effects cannot be excluded in an open-label responder-assessed design.

docs.title: Confidence — rationale
docs.text:
Show / hide

Strong proof-of-mechanism paper but I could only read the abstract on PMC (publisher restricts full text); the indication is cGVHD, not cancer, which caps its transferability.

docs.title: Retraction status
docs.text:
Show / hide

none

docs.title: Sallman DA 2023 — critique
docs.text:
Magrolimab in Combination With Azacitidine in Patients With Higher-Risk Myelodysplastic Syndromes: Final Results of a Phase Ib Study

← back to critique report · ← back to shieldbreak

PMID: 36888930 · PMCID: PMC10414740 · DOI: 10.1200/JCO.22.01794

Risk of bias (ROBINS-I): Moderate Per-trial confidence: Low Counter-productive MoA: Moderate (on-target-anemia, hematologic-stacking, homeostatic-clearance-disruption) Source tier: pmc_full_text

docs.title: Key critique
docs.text:
Show / hide

Phase 1b of magrolimab + azacitidine in untreated higher-risk MDS (n=95). 33% CR, 75% ORR, including 40% CR in TP53-mutant — a high-interest sub-population. Same caveat as Advani 2018: ex-vivo phagocytosis not measured; TAM biology is inferred. Anemia (on-target) managed via priming-dose strategy. The phase 3 ENHANCE trial subsequently reported futility and was stopped — context that reduces the forward-looking weight of this phase 1b.

docs.title: Per-dimension findings
docs.text: Show / hide

docs.title: Design
docs.text: Phase 1b, single-arm, open-label (NCT03248479). Magrolimab priming + ramp + 30 mg/kg maintenance weekly or q2w + azacitidine 75 mg/m² D1-7 of 28-day cycle. Primary endpoints: safety and CR. Formal χ² hypothesis test for CR rate at α=0.05 (rarely done in phase 1b — nice).

docs.title: Sample size
docs.text: n=95 treated. Larger than most phase 1b in this set. Adequate for CR-rate estimation and sub-group (TP53-mut n=25) exploration with appropriately wide CIs (40% CR, 95% CI not specified in abstract for subgroup).

docs.title: Effect magnitude calibration
docs.text: 33% CR / 75% ORR exceeds historical aza-monotherapy CR rates (~17-20%); however, ENHANCE-2 and ENHANCE-3 subsequently failed to demonstrate OS benefit. The phase 1b signal did not replicate in confirmatory phase 3 — important 'replication failure' context for this row.

docs.title: Missing data
docs.text: 91 of 95 discontinued by data cutoff; 30.5% progression, 5.3% lack of efficacy, 6-7% AE-related. 36% went to allo-HSCT (confounds duration-of-response analysis).

docs.title: Multiplicity
docs.text: Multiple CR / mCR / HI / MRD endpoints — descriptive mostly; formal test for primary CR.

docs.title: Generalizability
docs.text: HR-MDS, intermediate to very high risk; 26% TP53-mutant. Population is selected for organ function (GFR ≥40, ALT/AST ≤5x ULN).

docs.title: Gating / definition
docs.text: Bone marrow response by IWG 2006; MRD by multiparameter flow (0.02% LLOD). No direct TAM gating in bone marrow; mechanism is inferred from response to CD47 blockade + AZA (which upregulates calreticulin eat-me signal).

docs.title: Counter-productive mechanisms
docs.text: On-target anemia: Hb drop -0.7 g/dL median at first dose (range -3.1 to +2.4); 27.2% had ≥2 g/dL drop between doses 1-3. Priming-dose strategy largely mitigates the issue. Infusion-related reactions 5.3% grade 3. The phase 3 ENHANCE failures raise the possibility that CD47-blockade-driven anemia + AZA myelosuppression interact additively to worsen the cytopenia picture enough to offset leukemia-directed activity — a plausible counter-productive interaction at the full-trial scale.

docs.title: Conflict of interest & funding
docs.text: Gilead/Forty Seven funded. Extensive author disclosures including Gilead consulting; data supplement contains signed forms.

docs.title: Spin / framing
docs.text: Cautious in the discussion but the abstract's 'promising efficacy' framing preceded the phase 3 failures. Not spin at publication time; reads differently now.

docs.title: Extraction fidelity
docs.text:
Show / hide

No discrepancies noted — the screener's extracted values match the source.

docs.title: Risk of bias — rationale
docs.text:
Show / hide

Single-arm phase 1b with intact ITT analysis; allo-HSCT censoring is transparent.

docs.title: Confidence — rationale
docs.text:
Show / hide

For the TAM-biology question: mechanism inferred not measured, as user specifically flagged; plus phase 3 follow-on failures in the same mechanism/context reduce forward-looking weight.

docs.title: Retraction status
docs.text:
Show / hide

none

Phase 3 ENHANCE trial (in AML) reported futility in 2024; the MDS phase 3 ENHANCE trial also failed. Not a retraction but a significant negative follow-on that informs the weight of this paper.

docs.title: Hong DS 2023 — critique
docs.text:
Eganelisib, A First-In-Class PI3Kγ Inhibitor, in Patients with Advanced Solid Tumors: Results of the Phase 1/1b MARIO-1 Trial

← back to critique report · ← back to shieldbreak

PMID: 37000164 · PMCID: PMC10388696 · DOI: 10.1158/1078-0432.CCR-22-3313

Risk of bias (ROBINS-I): Moderate Per-trial confidence: Moderate Counter-productive MoA: Moderate (paradoxical-autoimmunity, neutrophil-function-impairment, dc-migration-impairment, hepatotoxicity) Source tier: pmc_full_text

docs.title: Key critique
docs.text:
Show / hide

Phase 1 dose-escalation of eganelisib (IPI-549, PI3Kγ inhibitor) in advanced solid tumors, monotherapy and with nivolumab (MARIO-1). PBMC PD readouts (MDSC reduction, CD8 activation gene signatures) with limited monotherapy efficacy; combination arm shows some activity. Well-designed PD study.

docs.title: Per-dimension findings
docs.text: Show / hide

docs.title: Design
docs.text: Phase 1 dose-escalation + expansion, two parts (eganelisib alone; eganelisib + nivolumab), NCT02637531. Open-label, single-arm each part.

docs.title: Sample size
docs.text: n=219 enrolled across monotherapy and combination; robust for PD analyses.

docs.title: Effect magnitude calibration
docs.text: Monotherapy efficacy negligible as expected for an immune-TME modulator. Combination activity concentrated in specific tumor types (e.g., MSS CRC, ICI-pretreated melanoma) is a reasonable signal but not definitive in single-arm design.

docs.title: Missing data
docs.text: Reported per protocol.

docs.title: Multiplicity
docs.text: Multiple biomarker comparisons; descriptive.

docs.title: Generalizability
docs.text: All-comer solid tumors with specific expansion cohorts.

docs.title: Gating / definition
docs.text: PBMC flow for MDSC subsets (HLA-DR^low CD11b+ CD33+) and monocyte-derived macrophage differentiation; on-treatment tumor biopsy in subset with mIF CD68/CD163.

docs.title: Counter-productive mechanisms
docs.text: PI3Kγ is expressed in multiple myeloid compartments (neutrophils, MDSCs, DCs, TAMs). Blockade reduces MDSC suppression — pro-anti-tumor — but also impairs neutrophil ROS-mediated tumor killing and DC migration/priming. Autoimmune hepatitis and colitis are reported PI3Kγ-class AEs. The drug's breadth is both its strength and its counter-productive risk.

docs.title: Conflict of interest & funding
docs.text: Infinity Pharmaceuticals funded; multiple employee co-authors.

docs.title: Spin / framing
docs.text: Measured; acknowledges limited monotherapy activity.

docs.title: Extraction fidelity
docs.text:
Show / hide

No discrepancies noted — the screener's extracted values match the source.

docs.title: Risk of bias — rationale
docs.text:
Show / hide

Standard phase 1 dose-escalation with solid PD characterization.

docs.title: Confidence — rationale
docs.text:
Show / hide

Full-text confirms target engagement and reasonable PD; clinical activity signal is cohort-dependent.

docs.title: Retraction status
docs.text:
Show / hide

none

docs.title: Coveler AL 2023 — critique
docs.text:
SEA-CD40, a non-fucosylated CD40 agonist: a phase 1 dose-escalation study

← back to critique report · ← back to shieldbreak

PMID: 37385724 · PMCID: PMC10314623 · DOI: 10.1136/jitc-2022-005584

Risk of bias (ROBINS-I): Moderate Per-trial confidence: Moderate Counter-productive MoA: Moderate (cytokine-release, thromboembolism, premature-t-cell-differentiation) Source tier: pmc_full_text

docs.title: Key critique
docs.text:
Show / hide

docs.title: Key critique
docs.text:
Show / hide

MATINS part 2 (translational update) of bexmarilimab (anti-CLEVER-1) in advanced solid tumors. Tumor TAM Clever-1+ reduction documented; macrophage activation markers shift. Program has pivoted heavily to hematologic malignancies (see Kontro 2025) after limited solid-tumor traction.

docs.title: Per-dimension findings
docs.text: Show / hide

docs.title: Design
docs.text: Multi-tumor phase 1/2 open-label single-arm (NCT03733990).

docs.title: Sample size

docs.title: Counter-productive mechanisms
docs.text: CLEVER-1 is expressed on tolerogenic hepatic sinusoidal endothelium and on tumor-associated lymphatic endothelium. Blocking CLEVER-1 in those compartments may disrupt normal tolerance/clearance functions (concern for autoimmunity and hepatic lipid-handling). Narrow-target relative to CSF1R/CCR2 — class-level counter-productive profile is genuinely less clear than other TAM-targeting approaches.

docs.title: Sun CM 2024 — critique
docs.text:
Impact of metronomic trabectedin and cyclophosphamide on the tumor microenvironment in refractory soft-tissue sarcoma patients (TARMIC study)

← back to critique report · ← back to shieldbreak

PMID: 38374062 · PMCID: PMC10875852 · DOI: 10.1186/s12943-024-01942-y

Risk of bias (ROBINS-I): Serious Per-trial confidence: Low Counter-productive MoA: Moderate (lymphopenia-collateral, systemic-cytotoxicity, steroid-premedication-immunosuppression) Source tier: pmc_full_text

docs.title: Key critique
docs.text:
Show / hide

TARMIC phase 1/2 metronomic trabectedin + low-dose cyclophosphamide in advanced STS. The primary efficacy endpoint (6-month non-progression 40% target) was NOT reached (observed 12.5%, 95% CI 2.7-32.4). TAM readout positive in a minority (9 of 28 paired biopsies with CD68+CD163+ reduction). Favorable immune shift correlated with improved PFS in an exploratory subgroup analysis — hypothesis-generating. The row should reflect the primary endpoint failure.

docs.title: Per-dimension findings
docs.text: Show / hide

docs.title: Design
docs.text: Phase 1/2 (NCT02406781), 3+3 dose-escalation → Simon 2-stage phase 2. n=50 (20 phase 1 + 30 phase 2). Central imaging review.

docs.title: Sample size
docs.text: n=28 with paired biopsies for TAM analysis. TAM reduction in 9/28 (32%). Subgroup analysis of responders vs non-responders has obvious selection bias.

docs.title: Effect magnitude calibration
docs.text: Primary endpoint missed. 'Up to 57% of patients exhibited a positive immunological response' is a composite of TAM-reduction OR CD8-increase — two separate criteria counted inclusively. That composite is a common way to inflate a biomarker success rate.

docs.title: Missing data
docs.text: 28 of 50 with paired biopsies (56%); the subgroup analyses effectively use this 28 as denominator. Authors disclose this.

docs.title: Multiplicity
docs.text: Multiple cutpoints for 'high/low' immune cell subsets computed by survminer 'optimal cut-point' — this is the worst kind of multiplicity (data-driven dichotomization of continuous biomarkers → inflated p-values). The authors label these analyses 'exploratory and hypothesis generating', which is the correct label.

docs.title: Generalizability
docs.text: Refractory advanced STS — narrow.

docs.title: Gating / definition
docs.text: CD68+CD163+ 2-plex IF with TSA + DAPI, Halo quantification. Reasonable gating; not multi-marker.

docs.title: Counter-productive mechanisms
docs.text: Trabectedin is a cytotoxic alkylating-like agent that depletes tumor cells AND monocytes/macrophages AND neutrophils — lymphopenia and cytopenias are dose-limiting. It is explicitly non-selective. Additive cyclophosphamide compounds lymphodepletion. The 'immune favorable' subset may simply be patients whose biology tolerated the cytotoxicity; the 'unfavorable' subset may have had lymphodepletion dominating. Steroid premedication (for trabectedin hepatotoxicity mitigation) adds further immunosuppression.

docs.title: Conflict of interest & funding
docs.text: Pharmamar + Institut National du Cancer. Authors disclose research grants.

docs.title: Spin / framing
docs.text: The abstract frames this as a positive paper ('positive immunological response…correlated with improved clinical benefit and progression-free survival') without emphasizing the primary-endpoint failure. The full text is more honest, but the framing is closer to spin than most papers in this set.

docs.title: Extraction fidelity
docs.text:
Show / hide

No discrepancies noted — the screener's extracted values match the source.

docs.title: Risk of bias — rationale
docs.text:
Show / hide

Missed primary endpoint + data-driven cutpoint subgroup analyses + open-label single-arm; the PD finding is hypothesis-generating only.

docs.title: Confidence — rationale
docs.text:
Show / hide

Full-text; primary endpoint not met and the positive PD claim rests on optimal-cutpoint subgroup analysis. The row should carry little weight.

docs.title: Retraction status
docs.text:
Show / hide

none

docs.title: Grierson PM 2025 — critique
docs.text:
Neoadjuvant BMS-813160 with Nivolumab and Gemcitabine plus Nab-Paclitaxel for Patients with Borderline Resectable or Locally Advanced Pancreatic Cancer

← back to critique report · ← back to shieldbreak

PMID: 40125795 · PMCID: PMC12402776 · DOI: 10.1158/1078-0432.CCR-24-1821

Risk of bias (ROBINS-I): Moderate Per-trial confidence: Moderate Counter-productive MoA: Moderate (bm-mobilization-suppression, t-cell-trafficking-disruption, monocyte-rebound) Source tier: pmc_full_text

docs.title: Key critique
docs.text:
Show / hide

Single-institution phase 1/2 of BMS-813160 (dual CCR2/5) + nivolumab + GnP in BR/LA PDAC with a small GnP-alone control arm. 22% ORR in the investigational arm (0% control); intratumoral monocyte/TAM reduction by scRNA-seq + flow. Negative 'sex as a biological variable not considered' statement and a COVID-era 2 deaths. Honest reporting; effect size modest and comparator small.

docs.title: Per-dimension findings
docs.text: Show / hide

docs.title: Design
docs.text: Single-institution phase 1/2 (NCT03496662). Part A: 1:1 random to investigational vs control (n=8 control). Part B: investigational only (n=25). Pre-specified primary endpoints were safety and ORR. scRNA-seq on biopsies with myeloid subclustering.

docs.title: Sample size
docs.text: n=32 investigational + 8 control. The 8-patient control cannot support robust efficacy comparison; the statistical case rests on historical control calibration (LAPACT, NEOLAP-AIO-PAK-0113).

docs.title: Effect magnitude calibration
docs.text: ORR 22% ITT / 42% per-protocol BR / 20% per-protocol LA — consistent with the 33.6% LAPACT ORR for GnP alone in LA-PDAC. The author-claimed 'numerically better' mPFS in LA-PDAC is a cross-study comparison with obvious confounding risks.

docs.title: Missing data
docs.text: 3 of 32 investigational non-evaluable (sudden death, two pre-cycle-1 withdrawals). Substantial COVID impact during part B with 2 deaths from bacterial pneumonia after COVID infection.

docs.title: Multiplicity
docs.text: Multiple endpoints (safety, ORR, resection, PFS, OS, scRNA subclusters) — no formal adjustment; descriptive.

docs.title: Generalizability
docs.text: Single-institution with specialist PDAC multidisciplinary board selection; hard to generalize.

docs.title: Gating / definition
docs.text: scRNA-seq myeloid clusters (CSF1R/C1QA/C1QB/FCGR3A/CD14/CSF3R) plus flow cytometry on paired PBMCs and tumor digests. Strong gating strategy.

docs.title: Counter-productive mechanisms
docs.text: The paper itself reports the key counter-productive finding: intratumoral monocyte/macrophage reduction with concurrent lack of change in peripheral-blood inflammatory monocytes — the authors interpret this as the investigational arm engaging target in tumor without fully suppressing BM-egress, but it could equally be read as BM-egress compensation. Combining CCR2/5 block with nivolumab: CCR5 blockade affects T-cell trafficking (Th1 homing via CCR5) — potentially counter-productive for anti-tumor effector recruitment.

docs.title: Conflict of interest & funding
docs.text: NIH + BMS collaborative; transparent disclosures per journal standards.

docs.title: Spin / framing
docs.text: Moderately overstated in the discussion ('prolonged PFS and OS in LA-PDAC patients, warranting a larger phase II study with a more efficacious CCR2-targeted therapeutic'). The 'more efficacious' phrasing concedes BMS-813160 under-delivered.

docs.title: Extraction fidelity
docs.text:
Show / hide

No discrepancies noted — the screener's extracted values match the source.

docs.title: Risk of bias — rationale
docs.text:
Show / hide

Partial randomization in part A is a design strength; small comparator n and single-institution setting are weaknesses.

docs.title: Confidence — rationale
docs.text:
Show / hide

Full text; scRNA-seq + flow evidence of intratumoral myeloid reduction is robust; efficacy claims need larger replication.

docs.title: Retraction status
docs.text:
Show / hide

none

docs.title: Daver N 2025 — critique
docs.text:
Azacitidine, Venetoclax, and Magrolimab in Newly Diagnosed and Relapsed Refractory Acute Myeloid Leukemia

← back to critique report · ← back to shieldbreak

PMID: 40198272 · PMCID: PMC12165814 · DOI: 10.1158/1078-0432.CCR-25-0229

Risk of bias (ROBINS-I): Moderate Per-trial confidence: Low Counter-productive MoA: High (hematologic-stacking, on-target-anemia, dose-intensity-failure) Source tier: pmc_full_text

docs.title: Key critique
docs.text:
Show / hide

Triplet magrolimab + aza + venetoclax in newly-diagnosed + R/R AML. Post-ENHANCE futility this paper is essentially the program's 'wind-down' combination-exploration dataset. Responses reported; TAM biology again inferred, not measured. Venetoclax adds its own myelosuppression to magrolimab-related anemia.

docs.title: Per-dimension findings
docs.text: Show / hide

docs.title: Design
docs.text: Phase 1/2 (NCT03248479 MDS stream or parallel AML study, details in full text). Triplet with decoupled magrolimab dosing schedules.

docs.title: Sample size
docs.text: AML-specific cohort size in full text.

docs.title: Effect magnitude calibration
docs.text: Activity reported in newly-diagnosed AML approximating other aza+ven regimens; the question is whether magrolimab adds value — answered 'no' at the phase 3 level (ENHANCE-3).

docs.title: Missing data
docs.text: Described in Methods.

docs.title: Multiplicity
docs.text: Multiple cohorts — descriptive.

docs.title: Generalizability
docs.text: AML newly-diagnosed (unfit) and R/R — two distinct populations pooled.

docs.title: Gating / definition
docs.text: Bone marrow response assessment; magrolimab RO in circulating cells. No direct TAM quantification.

docs.title: Counter-productive mechanisms
docs.text: Three-drug hematologic toxicity stacking: anemia (magrolimab on-target) + thrombocytopenia (venetoclax/magrolimab) + neutropenia (azacitidine/venetoclax). The cytopenia burden can force dose delays that reduce target engagement — a dose-intensity-based counter-productive pathway.

docs.title: Conflict of interest & funding
docs.text: Gilead/Forty Seven; extensive industry disclosures.

docs.title: Spin / framing
docs.text: Measured.

docs.title: Extraction fidelity
docs.text:
Show / hide

No discrepancies noted — the screener's extracted values match the source.

docs.title: Risk of bias — rationale
docs.text:
Show / hide

Standard phase 1/2 single-arm design.

docs.title: Confidence — rationale
docs.text:
Show / hide

TAM mechanism inferred; phase 3 failure in the same mechanism/context caps the weight this paper should carry.

Show / hide

docs.title: Kontro M 2025 — critique
docs.text:
Bexmarilimab plus azacitidine for high-risk myelodysplastic syndrome and relapsed or refractory acute myeloid leukaemia: a phase 1 trial

← back to critique report · ← back to shieldbreak

PMID: 40449509 · DOI: 10.1016/S2352-3026(25)00103-6

Risk of bias (ROBINS-I): Moderate Per-trial confidence: Low Counter-productive MoA: Unknown (hematologic-stacking, hepatic-endothelium-collateral) Source tier: pubmed_abstract

docs.title: Key critique
docs.text:
Show / hide

Phase 1 dose-escalation of bexmarilimab + azacitidine in HR-MDS / R/R AML (BEXMAB). RP2D 6.0 mg/kg in MDS arm. Encouraging preliminary activity; CR/ORR numbers in abstract. CLEVER-1 on myeloid leukemia cells adds rationale beyond TAM modulation.

docs.title: Per-dimension findings
docs.text: Show / hide

docs.title: Design
docs.text: Multicenter phase 1/2 (Finland + USA), dose-escalation phase reported, single-arm, NCT05428969. 33 patients (14 MDS, 19 R/R AML). Median follow-up 6.2 months.

docs.title: Sample size
docs.text: Adequate for phase 1 RP2D with 3+3-like dose-escalation.

docs.title: Effect magnitude calibration
docs.text: Activity comparison to aza-monotherapy in HMA-naïve MDS difficult without controls; abstract frames as 'promising.'

docs.text: Not extractable from abstract.

docs.title: Multiplicity
docs.text: Phase 1 — descriptive.

docs.title: Generalizability
docs.text: HR-MDS and R/R AML; specific to CLEVER-1 high-expression contexts.

docs.title: Gating / definition
docs.text: BM TAM gating not visible in abstract; blood monocyte PD likely.

docs.title: Counter-productive mechanisms
docs.text: CLEVER-1 class collateral (unknown-severity hepatic endothelium, scavenger-receptor effects). Stacked with aza myelosuppression. Unknown whether aza-induced hypomethylation of CLEVER-1 regulatory elements could produce paradoxical CLEVER-1 upregulation — not tested.

docs.title: Conflict of interest & funding
docs.text: Faron Pharmaceuticals funded. Multiple authors are current or former Faron employees; MFL/IP are former employees; JJ/MH hold shares. Transparent but pervasive sponsor involvement.

docs.title: Spin / framing
docs.text: Abstract's 'promising clinical activity' tempered by 'manageable safety profile' and 'MDS…with no response to hypomethylating agents' framing — honest.

docs.title: Strati P 2025 — critique
docs.text:
A Phase I Trial of Evorpacept, Lenalidomide, and Rituximab for Patients with B-Cell Non-Hodgkin Lymphoma

← back to critique report · ← back to shieldbreak

PMID: 40729376 · PMCID: PMC12370181 · DOI: 10.1158/1078-0432.CCR-25-1826

Risk of bias (ROBINS-I): Moderate Per-trial confidence: Low Counter-productive MoA: Moderate (on-target-thrombocytopenia, hematologic-stacking) Source tier: pmc_full_text

docs.title: Key critique
docs.text:
Show / hide

Phase 1 of evorpacept (ALX148, CD47 blocker engineered to minimize RBC binding) + lenalidomide + rituximab in R/R B-NHL. Evorpacept's main selling point is reduced on-target anemia versus magrolimab — partially borne out here. TAM biology inferred; no ex-vivo phagocytosis assay. Early activity signal in a small cohort.

docs.title: Per-dimension findings
docs.text: Show / hide

docs.title: Design
docs.text: Phase 1 open-label single-arm, dose-escalation of evorpacept + fixed lenalidomide + rituximab.

docs.title: Sample size
docs.text: Small phase 1.

docs.title: Effect magnitude calibration
docs.text: Responses in R/R B-NHL; cross-comparison to magrolimab + rituximab (Advani 2018) premature given sample size.

docs.title: Missing data
docs.text: Reported.

docs.title: Multiplicity
docs.text: Phase 1 — descriptive.

docs.title: Generalizability
docs.text: R/R B-NHL.

docs.title: Gating / definition
docs.text: Flow-based CD47 RO on circulating cells; no TAM quantification.

docs.title: Counter-productive mechanisms
docs.text: Evorpacept's engineered design reduces RBC binding so anemia is lower than magrolimab — but the 'don't eat me' signal disruption on nucleated cells remains. Platelet binding and on-target thrombocytopenia can still occur. Lenalidomide adds its own myelosuppression and teratogenicity constraints.
